title: [phase:plan] [family:approval-tokens] [provider_hint:codex] Current task: openclaw-tmux replacement. Review the new prototype and propose the minimum scoped approval-token model to replace coarse --approve. Return concise bullets: token fields, validation rules, TTL, and how to bind token to action+...
summary_text: **Token fields (minimum)** - `v` (schema version, `1`) - `jti` (UUID, single-use token id) - `iss` (approval issuer/service id) - `sub` (approved actor/user id) - `iat`, `exp` (issued/expiry UTC) - `action` (exact mutating action name) - `target_session`, `target_window`, `target_pane` (nullable but explicit) - `payload_hash` (SHA-256 of canonicalized mutation payload) - `reason` (human reason string) - `sig` (HMAC-SHA256 or JWS signature over all fields except `sig`) **Validation rules** - Mutating actions require `--approval-token`; remove boolean `--approve`. - Verify signature with trusted key; reject on parse/signature/version failure. - Enforce actor match: runtime actor must equal `sub`. - Enforce exact match on `action` and all target fields (including nulls). - Recompute `payload_hash` from normalized payload and require exact constant-time match. - Enforce time window: `iat <= now <= exp`. - Enforce one-time use: reject reused `jti` (atomic consume on success). - Keep existing pre-exec target revalidation (session/window/pane exists now) to fail closed on churn. - Audit allow/deny with `jti`, binding tuple, and validation failure reason. **TTL** - Default token TTL: `120s`. - Hard max accepted by validator: `300s` (`exp - iat`); longer tokens rejected. **Binding to action + target + payload** - Canonical binding object: `{v,jti,sub,action,target_session,target_window,target_pane,payload_hash,iat,exp,reason}`. - Sign canonical JSON (sorted keys, UTF-8) to produce `sig`. - At execution, rebuild canonical object from actual request/normalized payload, verify `sig`, then compare each bound field exactly. - Only execute if the full tuple matches and token is unexpired + unused.
